--- MODE SWITCH: PROGRESS SUMMARY ---
Do NOT output <observation> tags. This is a summary request, not an observation request.
Your response MUST use <summary> tags ONLY. Any <observation> output will be discarded.

PROGRESS SUMMARY CHECKPOINT
===========================
Write progress notes of what was done, what was learned, and what's next. This is a checkpoint to capture progress so far. The session is ongoing - you may receive more requests and tool executions after this summary. Write "next_steps" as the current trajectory of work (what's actively being worked on or coming up next), not as post-session future work. Always write at least a minimal summary explaining current progress, even if work is still in early stages, so that users see a summary output tied to each request.

Claude's Full Response to User:
Both files are in their final synthesized state. The architecture is complete and live:

- **`worldclass-dna.md`** — all 12 behavioral strands active, ambient in every session
- **`agent-dna-classifier/SKILL.md`** — 6-phase audit workflow (Scan→Extract→Classify→Research→Report→Apply), 137-skill routing registry, non-audit modes

The three-layer stack is operational:

```
~/.claude/rules/worldclass-dna.md     ← always on, 12 strands + quality floor
~/.claude/skills/agent-dna-classifier ← 6-phase audit, 137-skill routing
<specific skill>                      ← executes at world-class standard
```

To use the audit against your installed agents, invoke: `/agent-dna-classifier scan` — it will scan `~/.claude/agents/`, extract all behavioral directives, classify DNA vs Skills, identify missing strands, and wait for your approval before applying Phase 6 rewrites.

Respond in this XML format:
<summary>
  <request>[Short title capturing the user's request AND the substance of what was discussed/done]</request>
  <investigated>[What has been explored so far? What was examined?]</investigated>
  <learned>[What have you learned about how things work?]</learned>
  <completed>[What work has been completed so far? What has shipped or changed?]</completed>
  <next_steps>[What are you actively working on or planning to work on next in this session?]</next_steps>
  <notes>[Additional insights or observations about the current progress]</notes>
</summary>

IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this next PROGRESS SUMMARY - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the summary content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful summary content.

Thank you, this summary will be very useful for keeping track of our progress!

content.text:
<summary>
  <request>Integration assessment and synthesis of agent-dna-auditor repository with existing blender-ai-agent system for world-class performance</request>
  <investigated>Complete mapping of agent-dna-auditor repository (13 DNA strands, SKILL.md implementation, before-after transformation examples); existing worldclass-dna.md global baseline; agent-dna-classifier umbrella skill; architectural patterns distinguishing DNA (permanent methodology) from Tech Stack Skills (framework-specific capabilities); 6-phase workflow: Scan→Extract→Classify→Research (via EXA MCP)→Report→Apply</investigated>
  <learned>Agent-dna-auditor provides composable behavioral framework with 13 strands (adaptability, adversarial-thinking, completion-discipline, delegation-quality, learning-loops, output-quality, patience-discipline, protocol-awareness, requirements-discipline, session-hygiene, tool-mastery, writing-quality) plus core technical strands; Classification heuristic "Would this apply if project switched frameworks?" separates DNA from Skills; Behavioral strands define HOW to operate independent of tech stack; Global worldclass-dna.md provides always-active baseline inherited by all agents; Agent-specific DNA only captures unique characteristics, not repeating global standards; Three-layer stack architecture: worldclass-dna.md (quality floor) → agent-dna-classifier (audit/route/classify) → specific skills (execution)</learned>
  <completed>Integrated 12 behavioral strands into worldclass-dna.md (Writing Quality, Output Quality, Requirements Discipline, Adaptability, Completion Discipline, Session Hygiene, Patience Discipline, Protocol Awareness, Tool Mastery, Adversarial Thinking, Delegation Quality, Learning Loops); Refactored worldclass-dna.md from 122 to 202 lines with condensed writing following strand principles; Updated agent-dna-classifier/SKILL.md with 6-phase audit workflow implementation; Added DNA gap detection in Phase 3; Integrated EXA MCP research capability in Phase 4; Reorganized modes into audit/scan (full 6 phases) vs route/classify/embed-dna (simplified workflows); Established three-layer stack visualization showing inheritance model</completed>
  <next_steps>System ready for operational use; agent-dna-classifier can now audit existing agents via `/agent-dna-classifier scan` or `/agent-dna-classifier audit` commands to identify DNA vs Skills separation opportunities, detect missing behavioral strands, and propose rewrites with user approval; Integration synthesis complete with both core and executive functionalities preserved at world-class performance level</next_steps>
  <notes>The synthesis achieved complete integration without requiring separate installation of agent-dna-auditor - all capabilities (6-phase workflow, behavioral strands, classification heuristics, gap detection, research enhancement) are now embedded directly into the existing agent-dna-classifier skill and worldclass-dna.md baseline; The before-after sigma-backend.md examples demonstrated the transformation pattern: mixed inline methodology becomes separated DNA blocks with invokable skill references; Agent DNA Classifier now serves as both the auditor and the umbrella routing system for the 137-skill library</notes>
</summary>
